items.title: Jaya Grocer Launches ‘JS Cage-Free Eggs’ To Advance Animal Welfare
items.summary: With the introduction of JS Cage-Free Eggs, Jaya Grocer reinforces its role as a forward-thinking retailer committed to innovation, quality, and responsibility. The post Jaya Grocer Launches ‘JS Cage-Free Eggs’ To Advance Animal Welfare appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Junior Doctors Work 10 To 15 Overtime Hours Weekly Without Pay: MMI
items.summary: MMI says junior doctors in Malaysia work 10-15 unrecorded and unpaid overtime hours weekly. HOs or MOs in Malaysia average at 65-85 weekly working hours, compared to 40 hours in the UK. MMI urges MOH to formalise a Safe Working Hours Policy for doctors. The post Junior Doctors Work 10 To 15 Overtime Hours Weekly Without Pay: MMI appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Galen Centre Moots Health Service Commission With Ring-Fenced Five-Year Funding
items.summary: The Galen Centre calls for the establishment of a Health Service Commission (SPK) authorised to create and fill positions, and set salary scales, with funding for these positions ring-fenced in a 5-year fiscal framework. MPs have mixed views about the SPK. The post Galen Centre Moots Health Service Commission With Ring-Fenced Five-Year Funding appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Post-Maternity Wage Allowance In Malaysia: Breastfeeding, Work Retention, And Structural Conditions — June Joseph
items.summary: The return to work is where biological needs meet institutional design. If that interface is unsupported, even strong early breastfeeding initiation will struggle to continue. items.title: Government Backbenchers Tout Base MHIT To Mitigate Health Spending Cuts
items.summary: BBC chair Dr Zaliha Mustafa wants the Base MHIT to be launched quickly to mitigate Putrajaya's health spending cuts, so as to reduce dependence on public health care funded by the government. Base MHIT is meant to be offered by private insurance companies. The post Government Backbenchers Tout Base MHIT To Mitigate Health Spending Cuts appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Agriculture Ministry Braces For Potential Development Spending Cuts
items.summary: A senior KPKM official warns of potential cuts to agricultural development expenditure, such as irrigation systems, saying the money may be redirected to fund the country's mounting fuel subsidies. The post Agriculture Ministry Braces For Potential Development Spending Cuts appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Cut The Budget, Not The System: A Call For Health Care Stakeholders To Unite Now — Dr Raymond Choy
items.summary: Cost optimisation must never come at the expense of access or quality. Instead, it should drive smarter design, ensuring that care is delivered earlier, more efficiently, and in the most appropriate setting.
